Name: Source Note
Faye: The passages are adapted from Nathaniel Hawthorne, The Blithedale Romance. Originally published in 1852.
Five-and-twenty years ago, at the epoch of this story, there dwelt, a man whom we shall call Fauntleroy.
column3: Five-and-twenty years ago, there dwelt a man, named faulterton

Faye: A man of wealth, and magnificent tastes, and prodigal expenditure. His home might almost be styled a palace, his habits in the ordinary sense, princely.
column3: a man of wealth, and magmificant taste. His home expenditure styled a palace. habits homely, princely

Faye: His whole being seemed to have crystallized itself into an external splendor, wherewith he glittered in the eyes of the world.
column3: his whole being seemd to crystalized into splendor, wehrewith he gliterred into the world

Faye: He saw himself in imminent peril of losing all that had heretofore distinguished him; and, conscious of no innate worth to fall back upon, he recoiled from this calamity, with the instinct of a soul shrinking from annihilation.
column3: he saw himself in imminent pearl, conscious of no peril

Faye: Fauntleroy's guilt was discovered. He fled; his wife perished by the necessity of her innate nobleness, in its alliance with a being so ignoble; and betwixt her mother's death and her father's ignominy, his daughter was left worse than orphaned.
column3: fauntleroy's guilt was discovered. he fled, his wife perished by the necessity. his daughered was left as an orphan
